metadata.name: 5th Grade School Curriculum
metadata.description: Questions and concepts from the 5th grade curriculum — geography, American history, science, math, art, and sports.
metadata.author: Trivial World Team

questions.questionText: What is the capital of the United States?
Which ocean is the largest in the world?
On which continent is Brazil located?
What large canyon in Arizona was carved by the Colorado River?
questions.tidbits: It is about 277 miles long, up to 18 miles wide, and over a mile deep.

questions.questionText: Which US state is the largest in total land area?
What is the capital city of Mexico?
The Amazon River flows through which South American country?
questions.tidbits: The Amazon carries more water than any other river on Earth.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the body of water that separates Asia from North America?
What is the highest mountain in North America?
questions.answerText: Denali (also called Mount McKinley), located in Alaska

questions.questionText: What is the capital city of Canada?
What is the capital of France?
On which continent is Australia located?
What is the name of the world's longest mountain range, located in South America?
What large body of water borders the eastern coast of the United States?
What country shares the longest border with the United States?
What is the name of the five connected freshwater lakes on the US-Canada border?
What is the smallest continent by land area?
What imaginary line divides the Earth into the Northern and Southern Hemispheres?
What US state is made up of a chain of volcanic islands in the Pacific Ocean?
What is the tallest mountain in the world?
Who wrote the Harry Potter book series?
Who was the first president of the United States?
What is the name of the pig who is the main character in "Charlotte's Web"?
Which inventor is credited with creating the first practical electric light bulb?
Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his "I Have a Dream" speech in what year? A) 1955, B) 1963, C) 1968, D) 1971
Who painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel?
What woman is famous for leading enslaved people to freedom through the Underground Railroad?
In what year did the Wright Brothers make their first powered airplane flight? A) 1893, B) 1903, C) 1913, D) 1923
What pen name did author Samuel Clemens use when writing "The Adventures of Tom Sawyer"?
What author wrote the "Diary of a Wimpy Kid" series?
Who is the main character in the "Percy Jackson" series?
Who was the famous scientist who developed the theory of gravity after observing a falling apple?
What famous nurse, known as the founder of modern nursing, served during the Crimean War in the 1850s?
What is the name of the fictional wizard school Harry Potter attends?
What famous blind and deaf author wrote "The Story of My Life" and worked with teacher Anne Sullivan?
What scientist developed the telephone and is credited as its inventor in 1876?
What is the name of the spider in E.B. White's "Charlotte's Web" who saves Wilbur the pig?
Who was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize, winning two prizes in physics and chemistry?
What American poet wrote the famous poem that begins 'Two roads diverged in a yellow wood'?
What Indigenous leader and Shoshone woman helped Lewis and Clark navigate the western United States?
What document, signed in 1776, declared that the American colonies were free from British rule?
Who was president of the United States during the Civil War?
What year was the Declaration of Independence signed? A) 1765, B) 1776, C) 1787, D) 1800
What was the name of the ship that carried the Pilgrims to America in 1620?
What Native American woman helped guide Lewis and Clark on their westward expedition?
What invention by Eli Whitney greatly increased the demand for enslaved labor in the South?
questions.tidbits: Invented in 1793, the cotton gin made cotton farming so profitable that it dramatically expanded plantation slavery.

questions.questionText: What system of secret routes and safe houses helped enslaved people escape to freedom in the Northern states?
What act, passed in 1830, forced tens of thousands of Native Americans to relocate west of the Mississippi?
The first ten amendments to the U.S. Constitution are collectively called what?
What 1777 battle in New York is considered a turning point of the American Revolution because it convinced France to support the colonists?
What war was fought between the Northern (Union) and Southern (Confederate) states from 1861 to 1865?
In what year did Christopher Columbus first sail to the Americas? A) 1392, B) 1492, C) 1592, D) 1692
What amendment to the US Constitution abolished slavery in 1865?
What was the name of the first permanent English settlement in America, founded in 1607?
What famous tea protest in 1773 led colonists to dump British tea into Boston Harbor?
What document, written in 1787, established the basic laws and structure of the US government?
Who was the commander of the Continental Army during the American Revolution?
What 1803 land deal doubled the size of the United States by purchasing territory from France?
What famous speech given by Abraham Lincoln in 1863 honored soldiers who died at the Battle of Gettysburg?
What is the name of the trail followed by hundreds of thousands of settlers moving west from Missouri to Oregon in the 1800s?
What are the three primary colors in art?
Who painted the Mona Lisa?
What do you call the speed at which a piece of music is played?
What is the term for a painting done directly on wet plaster on a wall or ceiling?
questions.tidbits: The word "fresco" means "fresh" in Italian, referring to the wet plaster.

questions.questionText: What famous Dutch painter, known for cutting off part of his own ear, painted "The Starry Night"?
What technique creates art by gluing photos, paper, and other materials onto a flat surface?
What are the three secondary colors, each made by mixing two primary colors?
Which Renaissance sculptor created the famous marble statues "David" and "Pietà"?
What is the name of the traditional Japanese art of paper folding?
What American folk artist started painting at age 78 and became famous for colorful scenes of rural life?
What instrument family includes the piano, xylophone, and drums?
How many lines are in a standard musical staff?
What art term describes colors that are opposite each other on the color wheel, like red and green?
What famous American composer wrote many marches, including 'Stars and Stripes Forever'?
What is the name of the process of creating art by pressing ink from a carved block or plate onto paper?
What musical term means to play or sing gradually getting louder?
What French art movement, led by Claude Monet, is known for capturing fleeting light with short, visible brushstrokes?
What is the term for the way an artist arranges elements in a piece of art to create balance and visual flow?
What ancient Greek philosopher is credited with inventing the first musical scale and the theories behind music harmony?
What is the name of the famous painting by Grant Wood showing a man and woman standing in front of a farmhouse?
What planet in our solar system is known as the Red Planet?
What gas do plants produce during photosynthesis that animals need to breathe?
What is 3/4 + 1/4?
How many planets are in our solar system? A) 7, B) 8, C) 9, D) 10
questions.tidbits: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006, leaving eight official planets.

questions.questionText: What force pulls objects toward the surface of the Earth?
What type of rock is formed when lava or magma cools and hardens?
What is the process by which liquid water turns into water vapor and rises into the atmosphere?
What is the decimal equivalent of 3/8? A) 0.25, B) 0.30, C) 0.375, D) 0.5
What organelle in a cell is known as the "powerhouse" because it produces energy?
Which layer of the Earth's atmosphere protects life by absorbing most of the Sun's harmful ultraviolet radiation?
What is the closest star to Earth?
What is 25% of 80?
What three states of matter can water exist in?
questions.answerText: Solid (ice), liquid (water), and gas (steam or water vapor)

questions.questionText: What process do plants use to make their own food using sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide?
What is the name for the path a planet travels around the Sun?
If a rectangle has a length of 12 and a width of 4, what is its perimeter?
What is the largest planet in our solar system?
What tool do scientists use to classify living things into groups based on shared characteristics?
What is the term for the process by which water vapor in the air cools and turns back into liquid droplets to form clouds?
What is the boiling point of water in degrees Celsius?
How many players from each team are on the court in a basketball game at one time? A) 4, B) 5, C) 6, D) 7
In soccer, what role is the only player allowed to use their hands?
How many innings are in a standard baseball game? A) 7, B) 8, C) 9, D) 10
What sport uses a shuttlecock instead of a ball?
How often do the Summer Olympic Games take place? A) Every 2 years, B) Every 4 years, C) Every 6 years, D) Every 8 years
In gymnastics, what piece of equipment is a long, narrow elevated beam that gymnasts balance and perform on?
How many points is a touchdown worth in American football? A) 3, B) 6, C) 7, D) 8
In volleyball, how many players from each team are on the court at one time? A) 4, B) 5, C) 6, D) 7
What fitness principle says that you must gradually increase the difficulty of exercise over time to keep improving?
What famous annual tennis tournament is played on grass courts in Wimbledon, England?
How many bases are there on a baseball field?
In basketball, how many points is a shot worth if made from behind the three-point line?
What is the term for the starting position in a race where runners crouch down behind the starting line?
In soccer, how many players does each team have on the field during a standard match?
What is the name of the swimming stroke where both arms are pulled back simultaneously like a butterfly?
In gymnastics, what event requires a gymnast to swing and release between two bars at different heights?
What is the name of the track-and-field relay event where four runners each run 100 meters and pass a baton?
How many events make up the Olympic decathlon?
What is the official length of a marathon race?
In American football, how many yards does the offense need to gain in four downs to keep possession of the ball?
